upgradeFromMmw.title: Migrate from MiaoMiaoWu to MiaoMiaoWu X
upgradeFromMmw.description: Fully migrate existing MiaoMiaoWu (mmw) data to MiaoMiaoWu X, preserving client subscription URLs / xray credentials / nginx / certificates, only replacing the master

demo.heading: Full migration wizard (demo)
demo.intro: Below is a local mock of the real master /migrate-from-mmw page — all 5 steps, every interaction, form, and scan result is clickable, but everything is mocked and no network call is made. Walk through it here to get familiar before doing it on the real master.

whoNeeds.heading: Who Needs This
whoNeeds.intro: Already using MiaoMiaoWu (mmw) for subscription + node management, wanting to upgrade to MiaoMiaoWu X (mmwx) but don't want to:
whoNeeds.item1: Have clients re-add subscriptions (URL stays unchanged)
whoNeeds.item2: Re-apply SSL certificates / change nginx reverse proxy config
whoNeeds.item3: Modify xray server UUID / password (protocol layer transparent)
whoNeeds.item4: Manually rebuild packages / users / nodes / subscription templates
whoNeeds.conclusion: This migration wizard is designed for you. All operations only touch the mmw database, not clients, not nginx, not certificates. Failure is rollbackable.

guarantees.heading: Migration Guarantees vs Prerequisites
guarantees.g1: Client subscription URL unchanged -- Clash / Shadowrocket etc. don't need changes
guarantees.g2: xray UUID / password unchanged -- protocol layer transparent
guarantees.g3: nginx config untouched -- reverse proxy continues pointing to same port
guarantees.g4: SSL certificate untouched -- certbot doesn't need re-application
guarantees.g5: mmw service rollbackable -- systemctl only stops, doesn't delete any files
guarantees.g6: User short codes / subscription short codes preserved -- previously distributed /x/<code> short links continue working
guarantees.p1: Have saved a complete data backup of MiaoMiaoWu
guarantees.p2: Have installed mmwx binary (direct install or Docker) and running on the same port as mmw via systemd
guarantees.p3: Current mmwx database is empty (fresh install state, no packages / nodes / users), otherwise mmw data will overwrite
guarantees.p4: Have root SSH access to the mmw deployment machine
guarantees.p5: All remote node servers used during mmw era have mmw-agent installed and connected to mmwx master, see Agent Deployment
guarantees.p6: Clients will experience a 1-2 minute brief disconnection during migration (mmw stops -> mmwx takes over subscription)

entry.heading: Entry Point
entry.text: After logging into mmwx with an admin account, click the avatar menu in the top right -> Migrate from MiaoMiaoWu to enter the 5-step wizard. This entry is invisible to regular users. All 6 /api/admin/migrate/* endpoints are protected by RequireAdmin middleware; non-admin calls return 403.
entry.time: Estimated total time: 10-30 minutes (mainly depends on mmw data size and SSH network).

step1.heading: Step 1 -- Overview & Prerequisites
step1.text: The wizard homepage lists the guarantees + prerequisites above. Please review carefully -- especially the 'mmwx database must be empty' requirement, otherwise continuing will fail or overwrite existing data. Confirm and click 'I understand, begin'.

step2.heading: Step 2 -- Stop mmw + Backup
step2.auto1: Enter mmw master address, admin username / password
step2.auto2: mmwx master logs into mmw with that account, calls mmw's /api/admin/backup/download to pull a complete backup zip (database + subscription files) to mmwx local /tmp/mmwx-migrate/
step2.auto3: Extract, record db path, subscription file count, show for confirmation
step2.auto4: SSH to mmw machine, systemctl stop miaomiaowu to stop service, preventing mmw data changes during migration
step2.manualText: Don't want to give mmw credentials / can't run SSH? Switch to manual mode, the wizard provides complete command snippets:
step2.manualNote: Copy the zip to any path on the mmwx master, tell the wizard the path in the next step.
step2.rollbackText: This step only does systemctl stop, doesn't delete any files. If anything goes wrong, SSH in and systemctl start miaomiaowu to restore mmw.

step3.heading: Step 3 -- Import mmw Database
step3.importAuto: Auto mode continuation: Step 2 already pulled backup locally, path is shown here, click Import
step3.importUpload: Upload zip: Browser upload the entire mmw backup zip (database + subscribes/), backend auto-extracts
step3.does1: Uses SQLite ATTACH DATABASE to mount mmw.db as src, each table INSERT OR IGNORE INTO main.X SELECT ... FROM src.X
step3.does2: Migrated tables: users, user_tokens, nodes, subscribe_files, user_subscriptions, user_settings, templates, custom_rules, override_scripts, external_subscriptions
step3.does3: User short codes + subscription short codes preserved as-is (from user_tokens / subscribe_files), previously distributed /x/<code> short links continue working
step3.does4: Copy yaml template files from subscribes/ directory to mmwx subscribes/ (same-name files keep existing mmwx version, no overwrite)
step3.does5: Set created_by to the first admin username for subscribe_files / templates rows where created_by is empty
step3.transactionNote: The entire import runs in one SQLite transaction. If any table fails -> complete rollback, database stays in pre-import state.

step4.heading: Step 4 -- Claim Nodes / Users
step4.intro: mmw-era node configs exist as clash_config (raw vless / vmess / trojan / hy2 configs) without a 'remote server' concept -- servers were manually maintained. mmwx uses a 'Server + Inbound + Node' three-layer model, requiring mmw nodes to be linked to mmwx managed xray servers. This step does two things:
step4.addServer: Add Servers -- The wizard lists all deduplicated server addresses from mmw nodes. Click 'Go Add' to auto-create a remote server in mmwx (address / name auto-filled from node info), generate agent token, and prompt you to install mmw-agent on the server.
step4.takeoverXray: Take Over External xray + Scan to Fill Emails -- For externally managed xray from the mmw era, if choosing embedded mode redeployment, one-click merge /etc/xray/config.json + /etc/xray/conf/*.json to mmwx standard path /usr/local/etc/xray/config.json (originals archived as .before-mmwx-<ts>). Then scan all inbound clients in the xray config, fill in emails and bind to admin's sub-accounts (mmw era didn't have this concept; after migration, these 'custom clients' are unified under admin).
step4.smartClaimText: After scanning, the master syncs xray inbounds to the mmwx node table. If an inbound's protocol:port matches an already-imported 'external node' (original_server='') from mmw, it automatically claims that node (binds server + inbound_tag) instead of creating a duplicate. Result shown as toast: 'Scan complete, auto-bound X existing nodes, added Y new nodes'.

step5.heading: Step 5 -- Verify & Complete
step5.verify1: Client subscription works -- Refresh a subscription URL from an original mmw user (unchanged) in Clash, verify nodes are still there and connectable.
step5.verify2: Node speed test -- Open the node speed test workbench in Node Management, test a few migrated nodes to confirm they work.
step5.verify3: User login -- Try logging into mmwx with a regular user account using the original mmw password, check if the personal subscription page is accessible.
step5.conclusion: All three pass, migration complete. Keep the mmw binary / data directory, clean up after 1-2 weeks of stable operation.

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.q1: Import shows 'INSERT OR IGNORE skipped N rows'
faq.a1: The mmwx database is not empty; some primary key / unique index conflicts caused rows to be skipped. Recommendation: stop mmwx -> delete data/mmwx.db -> restart mmwx (will re-run setup to create a clean database) -> re-run migration.
faq.a2: mmw's /api/admin/backup/download requires mmw v0.7+. Older versions can only use manual mode scp.
faq.q3: Some inbounds lost after taking over external xray
faq.a3: Merge logic deduplicates by tag field. Same-named tags in two confdir files keep only the first merged. If lost, restore from /etc/xray/config.json.before-mmwx-<ts> and manually merge into /usr/local/etc/xray/config.json.
faq.q4: Same server:port mmw external node created twice, no auto-binding
faq.a4: Claim logic matches by server + port + protocol triple. clash type:ss and xray protocol:shadowsocks equivalence is handled. If still unmatched, usually the mmw node's stored server address differs from mmwx server config (e.g., one uses IP, the other domain). Manually edit original_server / inbound_tag in node details.
faq.q5: Can regular users call migration APIs?
faq.a5: No. All 6 /api/admin/migrate/* endpoints are protected by RequireAdmin middleware (validates token + checks user.Role == 'admin'). Non-admins get 403. The frontend menu entry is also admin-only, and the /migrate-from-mmw route's beforeLoad guard redirects non-admins to the homepage.

rollback.heading: Rollback Plan
rollback.intro: Any step that goes wrong can be reverted to mmw:
rollback.step1: SSH to mmw machine: systemctl stop miaomiaowux && systemctl start miaomiaowu
rollback.step2: Original nginx reverse proxy config doesn't need changes -- it points to the same port, whoever starts up takes over
rollback.step3: SSL certificates / client subscription URLs all stay as-is, clients seamlessly switch back to mmw
rollback.step4: Clean up mmwx residual data: rm /path/to/mmwx/data/mmwx.db (next restart rebuilds empty database)
rollback.conclusion: Therefore migration is a low-risk operation: even if it goes wrong, you can switch back in 30 seconds.

xrayInbounds.title: Xray Inbound Management
xrayInbounds.description: Create and manage Xray inbound configurations using the inbound wizard
xrayInbounds.scopeAlertTitle: This page only manages Xray inbounds — it's NOT the node management page
xrayInbounds.scopeAlertText: Node operations (add / enable / disable / delete / rename) belong to
xrayInbounds.wizardText: The inbound wizard provides a visual configuration flow, guiding you through protocol, transport, security layer, and parameter selection to automatically generate complete Xray inbound configurations.
xrayInbounds.wizardStep1: 1. Select protocol: VLESS / VMess / Trojan / Shadowsocks / Hysteria2
xrayInbounds.wizardStep2: 2. Select transport: TCP / WebSocket / gRPC / XHTTP
xrayInbounds.wizardStep3: 3. Select security layer: TLS / REALITY / None / XTLS-Vision
xrayInbounds.combinationsText: Different protocols support different transport and security layer combinations. For the detailed combination matrix, see
xrayInbounds.op1: Inbounds auto-sync as nodes after creation (viewable in Node Management)
xrayInbounds.op2: Deleting an inbound auto-deletes the corresponding node
xrayInbounds.op3: View complete JSON configuration of inbounds
xrayInbounds.op4: Filtering: auto-hides API inbounds and runtime inbounds with empty tags
xrayInbounds.autoSyncText: Inbound creation/deletion automatically syncs to the node table via event bus. The sync process automatically converts Xray inbound configurations to mihomo/Clash compatible proxy configuration format.

xrayOutbounds.title: Xray Outbound Management
xrayOutbounds.description: Manage Xray outbound configurations and routing rules
xrayOutbounds.configText: Outbounds define how traffic leaves Xray. Default includes freedom (direct) and blackhole (block) outbounds.
xrayOutbounds.freedomDesc: Direct outbound, traffic sent directly to target
xrayOutbounds.proxyDesc: Proxy outbound, traffic forwarded through another proxy server
xrayOutbounds.warpDesc: Cloudflare WARP egress (WireGuard). One-click install from "Add Outbound → Cloudflare WARP"; each agent gets its own WARP account for traffic isolation
xrayOutbounds.routingText: Routing rules determine how inbound traffic is distributed to different outbounds. Can route based on domain, IP, protocol, and other conditions.
xrayOutbounds.operationsText: Managed in the Outbound and Routing tabs on the server details page. Supports adding, editing, and deleting outbounds and routing rules. Xray service restart required after changes.
xrayOutbounds.warpIntro: Since v0.2.3, miaomiaowuX ships one-click Cloudflare WARP integration. Each agent registers its own WARP account directly via the Cloudflare API (no wgcf binary required) and generates two WireGuard outbounds, warp-v4 and warp-v6, ready to be targeted from routing rules.
xrayOutbounds.warpStep1: 1. Under the Outbound tab click "Add Outbound" → "Cloudflare WARP" to open the WARP panel.
xrayOutbounds.warpStep2: 2. Click "Install WARP". The agent registers with Cloudflare, writes warp.json, and adds the warp-v4 + warp-v6 outbounds; the server card shows an orange W badge.
xrayOutbounds.warpStep3: 3. To upgrade to WARP+, paste the license key and click "Upgrade WARP+". "Refresh Config" re-pulls and re-injects the outbounds idempotently (same tag, no duplicates).
xrayOutbounds.warpAntiChinaText: On servers with WARP installed, the routing panel's "Quick rules" dropdown gains an "Anti-CN-Routing" entry: one click routes geosite:google + geosite:meta through warp-v4, preventing Google/Meta traffic from being routed via Chinese exits.
xrayOutbounds.warpNote1: Default MTU is 1420 (WireGuard standard). noKernelTun=false forces userspace gVisor TUN, so it works without host tun module / CAP_NET_ADMIN.
xrayOutbounds.warpNote2: Uninstall removes the Cloudflare account, deletes warp.json on the host, and drops warp-v4 / warp-v6 from xray in one shot.

xrayRouting.title: Xray Routing Management
xrayRouting.description: Manage Xray routing rules, control traffic forwarding policies
xrayRouting.overviewText: The Xray routing module uses first-match semantics: rules are matched top to bottom, stopping at the first hit. Unmatched traffic goes to the default outbound (outbounds[0]). MiaoMiaoWu X provides two entry points for managing routing rules:
xrayRouting.entryNodeRoutingDesc: View and manage routing rules for a single node (inbound)
xrayRouting.entryRoutingPanelDesc: Manage all server routing rules with drag-and-drop sorting
xrayRouting.matchSemanticsIntro: Xray routing rules follow these semantics:
xrayRouting.matchRule1: Rules match top to bottom, stopping at first hit (first-match)
xrayRouting.matchRule2: Multiple conditions in a single rule are AND (e.g., domain + protocol must both match)
xrayRouting.matchRule3: A rule with only inboundTag + outboundTag and no other conditions = catch-all, matching 100% of that inbound's traffic
xrayRouting.matchRule4: Global rules after catch-all no longer apply to that inbound
xrayRouting.matchRule5: When no rule matches, traffic goes to outbounds[0] (default outbound)
xrayRouting.matchOrderNote: Therefore, rule order is critical. More specific rules should come first, catch-all rules last.
xrayRouting.nodeRoutingText: On the node management page, each remote server node has a routing button. Click to open the node routing dialog.
xrayRouting.exclusiveGlobalIntro: The dialog categorizes routing rules into two types:
xrayRouting.exclusiveRule: Exclusive rules: Rules containing the current node's inboundTag, only effective for this inbound
xrayRouting.globalRule: Global rules: Rules without inboundTag, effective for all inbounds
xrayRouting.catchAllText: If a catch-all rule exists in exclusive rules (only inboundTag + outboundTag, no domain/ip/protocol conditions), the system auto-hides the global rules and default outbound sections, showing a warning:
xrayRouting.catchAllWarning: All traffic has been routed to [outboundTag], subsequent global rules and default outbound no longer take effect
xrayRouting.outboundNameResolveText: The outboundTag in routing rules is automatically resolved to the corresponding node name. The system maps by matching the server:port in outbound config with the node's clash_config address.
xrayRouting.routingPanelText: In the Routing Tab of the Xray server management page, you can manage all routing rules for the remote server.
xrayRouting.splitLayoutIntro: The routing panel uses a left-right split design:
xrayRouting.splitLeft: Left (40%): Rule list, drag-and-drop sortable, click to select
xrayRouting.splitRight: Right (60%): Selected rule detailed fields + JSON preview + delete button
xrayRouting.dragSortText: Since Xray routing uses first-match semantics, rule order directly affects matching results. Drag the handle on the left side of rule cards to reorder. Changes auto-save and restart Xray on release.
xrayRouting.dragSortApi: Sorting is implemented via action: 'set' to fully replace routing rules. API rules (outboundTag = api) are automatically kept at the top.
xrayRouting.shortcutRulesText: Built-in common quick rules that can be added with one click:
xrayRouting.ruleBanCNDesc: Block access to mainland China IPs
xrayRouting.ruleBanPrivateDesc: Block access to private network addresses
xrayRouting.balancerText: Create a balancer to distribute traffic matching a routing rule across a group of outbounds, enabling multi-landing distribution / failover. Creation is available in Service Management routing config and Node Management routing dialog.
xrayRouting.balancerStep1: 1. Click Create Load Balancer in routing config.
xrayRouting.balancerStep2: 2. Use outbound prefix (selector) to select a group of outbounds (matched by tag prefix).
xrayRouting.balancerStep4: 4. Add a routing rule pointing balancerTag to the balancer (not a single outboundTag).
xrayRouting.balancerStep5: 5. When creating from node management routing dialog, default rule's inboundTag = node's TAG, can be changed to apply to all nodes.
xrayRouting.strategyLeastPing: Select lowest latency outbound (requires observation, auto-enables observatory)
xrayRouting.balancerObservatoryNote: When selecting leastPing / leastLoad, the system auto-configures observatory / burstObservatory for candidate outbound probing; random / roundRobin don't need probing.
xrayRouting.customRulesText: Custom rules support all Xray routing fields. Empty fields are not submitted. Multiple values separated by commas.
xrayRouting.domainFieldDesc: Domain matching, supports geosite:, domain:, full:, regexp:
xrayRouting.ipFieldDesc: IP matching, supports geoip:, CIDR, plain IP
xrayRouting.autoRestartText: After adding, deleting, or reordering routing rules on remote servers, the system automatically restarts Xray to apply changes. A toast notification is shown after completion.

xrayService.title: Xray Service Management
xrayService.description: Remote install/uninstall Xray and Nginx, service start/stop control
xrayService.xrayInstallText: On the Service Management page, click the Install Xray button on the server card.
xrayService.xrayInstall1: Installation progress displayed via SSE streaming in real-time
xrayService.xrayInstall2: Auto-downloads latest Xray-core and configures as system service
xrayService.xrayInstall3: Auto-triggers certificate deployment after installation (if configured)
xrayService.xrayInstall4: Auto-scans and syncs inbounds to node table after installation
xrayService.nginxInstallText: Nginx is used for TLS camouflage, forwarding port 443 traffic to Xray.
xrayService.nginxInstall1: If the server has a domain configured, Nginx reverse proxy is auto-configured during installation
xrayService.opRestartDesc: Restart Xray/Nginx service (required after config changes)
xrayService.opScanDesc: Scan service status and version info, sync inbounds to nodes
xrayService.configFileText: View and edit Xray/Nginx configuration files directly on the server details page. Supports multi-config file list, online editing and saving. Restart the corresponding service after config changes.

xraySystemConfig.title: Xray System Config
xraySystemConfig.description: Xray global configuration: log, DNS, policy, etc.
xraySystemConfig.overviewText: Xray system configuration contains global-level settings that affect all inbound and outbound behavior. Manageable in the System Config tab on the server details page.
xraySystemConfig.note1: Restart Xray service after modifying system config
xraySystemConfig.note2: Enabling traffic statistics slightly increases memory usage
xraySystemConfig.note3: API inbound is for gRPC traffic statistics, do not delete

systemSettings.title: System Settings
systemSettings.description: Detailed explanation of each MiaoMiaoWu X system setting
systemSettings.overviewText: The system settings page contains four main configuration areas: external subscription sync settings, feature toggles, notification push configuration, and proxy group config sync. All settings take effect immediately after modification, no service restart required.
systemSettings.adminOnly: System settings are visible only to admins. Regular users cannot access this page.
systemSettings.externalSubText: Configure sync behavior for external subscriptions (airport subscriptions). When external subscription links are imported, these settings control how external nodes are fetched and updated.
systemSettings.syncTrafficDesc: When enabled, traffic info page data includes external subscription traffic. The system reads the subscription-userinfo response header when fetching subscriptions and merges traffic data into total statistics.
systemSettings.appendSubInfoHeading: Append Subscription Info to Node Names
systemSettings.appendSubInfoDesc: When enabled, remaining traffic and days are appended to node names during external subscription sync. Example: NodeName 398.22GB 26Days. Convenient for users to view subscription traffic and validity directly in clients.
systemSettings.nodeFilterDesc: Uses regex to match node names. Matched nodes are filtered out during sync. Commonly used to filter traffic info nodes, announcement nodes, and other non-proxy nodes from airport subscriptions. Leave empty for no filtering.
systemSettings.externalSyncDesc: When enabled, the system re-fetches the latest nodes from external subscription links every time a user gets a subscription, updating the database. When disabled, only uses nodes already saved in the database.
systemSettings.externalSyncWarning: Enabling increases subscription API response time since external subscription links must be accessed each time. Recommended to use with cache TTL.
systemSettings.externalSyncSubConfig: When enabled, the following sub-configurations expand:
systemSettings.matchRuleDesc: Controls how local and remote nodes are matched during sync.
systemSettings.matchByNameDesc: Match by node name (default), suitable for subscriptions with stable node names
systemSettings.matchByServerPortDesc: Match by server address and port, suitable for subscriptions with frequently changing names
systemSettings.matchByTypeServerPortDesc: Match by protocol type + server + port, most precise matching
systemSettings.syncScopeDesc: Controls which nodes are updated during sync.
systemSettings.syncSavedDesc: Only update nodes already in the database (default), won't auto-add new nodes
systemSettings.syncAllDesc: Sync all nodes from remote subscription, new nodes auto-added to database
systemSettings.keepNameDesc: When enabled, preserves database node names during sync instead of using external subscription names. Suitable when node names have been manually modified.
systemSettings.cacheTTLDesc: Set to 0 to re-fetch external subscription on every subscription request. When > 0, only re-fetches if the set minutes have passed since last fetch. Recommended 30-60 minutes to balance freshness and response speed.
systemSettings.featureTogglesText: Manage the enabled status of system feature modules.
systemSettings.silentModeDesc: When enabled, MiaoMiaoWu X returns 404 pages for all requests, hiding the panel's existence. When a user fetches a subscription via subscription link, the server resumes normal access for a duration set by Recovery Duration. After timeout, it re-enters silent mode.
systemSettings.silentModeWarning: After enabling silent mode, you cannot directly access the panel. Only fetching a subscription can temporarily restore access. Ensure subscription links are properly configured before enabling.
systemSettings.recoveryDurationDesc: Duration of normal access after a user fetches a subscription. After timeout, re-enters 404 silent mode.
systemSettings.shortLinkDesc: When enabled, the subscription link page shows an additional 6-character short link. Short links are easier to share and type. Users can reset short links in personal settings.
systemSettings.clientCompatDesc: When enabled, subscription generation auto-filters node types unsupported by the target client (e.g., WireGuard nodes unsupported by Clash), only logging instead of erroring. When disabled, incompatible nodes produce error messages.
systemSettings.overrideScriptDesc: When enabled, the override management page shows script functionality. Use JavaScript scripts to modify subscription configs, such as changing node properties, adding custom fields, adjusting proxy group structure, etc. For users with advanced customization needs.
systemSettings.probeBindDesc: When enabled, each node in the node management list shows a probe binding button, allowing nodes to be bound to specific probe servers. Once bound, traffic statistics only aggregate probe data for that node, enabling more precise traffic attribution.
systemSettings.proxyProviderDesc: Proxy Provider allows dynamically loading nodes from external subscriptions. When enabled, proxy providers can be configured on the subscription files page and dragged into proxy groups during editing. Suitable for mixed use of local and external airport nodes.
systemSettings.templateVersionDesc: Select the template engine version used for subscription generation. Different versions have different template syntax and capabilities.
systemSettings.v1Desc: Uses file templates from rule_templates directory, direct YAML file editing
systemSettings.v2Desc: Uses database-stored templates, supports web-based visual management, compatible with universal backend template format
systemSettings.v3Desc: New template system, mihomo-like config style, supports visual proxy group and rule editing
systemSettings.subResponseHeaderDesc: When enabled, the system reads probe and external subscription traffic data during subscription fetch and writes subscription-userinfo info to HTTP response headers. Clients (like Clash, Stash) display traffic usage and expiry accordingly. When disabled, skips traffic data reading and doesn't write response headers.
systemSettings.subSerializationDesc: Select Clash subscription output format. Default YAML. When JSON is selected, Clash subscriptions output in JSON format. This setting only affects Clash format subscriptions, not other client formats (Surge, Sing-Box, Shadowrocket, etc.).
systemSettings.subInfoNodeDesc: When enabled, two info nodes are added at the top of the node list during subscription output, showing expiry time and remaining traffic. These are not real proxy nodes, only for displaying subscription info in client node lists.
systemSettings.subInfoNodeCustomize: When enabled, you can customize info node prefix text:
systemSettings.notificationText: Push system critical event notifications via Telegram Bot.
systemSettings.enableNotificationDesc: When enabled, the system sends event notifications via Telegram Bot. First configure Bot Token and Chat ID by clicking the gear icon next to the notification push toggle.
systemSettings.tgBotConfigText: Click the gear icon next to the notification push toggle to expand the config panel.
systemSettings.botTokenDesc: API Token obtained after creating a Bot from @BotFather
systemSettings.chatIdDesc: Telegram chat ID to receive notifications, can be personal, group, or channel
systemSettings.testNotificationNote: After configuration, click Send Test Notification to verify the setup.
systemSettings.notifyEventsText: Each notification event can be independently toggled:
systemSettings.eventSubFetchDesc: Send notification when user fetches subscription, includes username, client type, and IP
systemSettings.eventLoginDesc: Send notification when user logs into panel, includes username and IP
systemSettings.eventIpBanDesc: Send notification when IP is auto-banned due to multiple bad requests
systemSettings.eventSilentModeDesc: Send notification on silent mode status changes (entering/exiting 404 state)
systemSettings.eventSubExpiryDesc: Send reminder when user subscription is about to expire
systemSettings.eventDailyTrafficDesc: Send daily traffic statistics report. When enabled, set send time (default 08:00) in 24-hour HH:MM format
systemSettings.proxyGroupSyncDesc: Sync the latest preset proxy group config from a remote address. Proxy group config contains common rule categories and corresponding rule-providers settings. After sync, updates the rule selector and preset proxy groups on the subscription generation page.
systemSettings.remoteConfigAddrDesc: Customize the remote config URL. Leave empty to use system default or environment variable configured address.
systemSettings.proxyGroupSyncOp: Click Sync Proxy Group Config button to manually trigger sync. A confirmation prompt appears after successful sync.
systemSettings.masterConfigText: Master service configuration is set via config file or environment variables, independent of Web UI system settings.
systemSettings.masterConfigNote: Config file specified via -c config.yaml, can also be overridden by environment variables.
systemSettings.masterJwtSecret: JWT signing key for user authentication
systemSettings.masterLogLevel: System log level (debug / info / warn / error)
systemSettings.masterEnvText: Environment variables override same-named config in config.yaml:
systemSettings.agentConfigText: Agent is deployed on remote proxy servers and needs to configure the connection to Master.
systemSettings.agentRestartMethod: Xray restart method (auto / systemctl / custom command)
systemSettings.agentRestartCommand: Custom restart command (effective when restart_method is not auto)
systemSettings.connectionModeText: Agent and Master support multiple communication methods. Choose the most suitable mode based on your network environment.
systemSettings.modeAutoDesc: Automatically selects the best connection method. Prioritizes WebSocket, falls back to HTTP, then Pull mode. Supports exponential backoff auto-reconnection.
systemSettings.modeWsDesc: Full-duplex WebSocket connection with real-time bidirectional communication. Heartbeat interval 30 seconds, idle timeout 5 minutes. Suitable for stable networks.
systemSettings.modeHttpDesc: Actively pushes data to Master via HTTP POST. Suitable for environments that cannot establish persistent connections.
systemSettings.modePullDesc: Passive mode, Master actively pulls Agent data. Agent only exposes local API, no need to actively connect to Master. Suitable when Agent is behind NAT or cannot make outbound connections.
systemSettings.agentEnvText: Environment variables override same-named config in config.yaml:

sync.title: Subscription sync
sync.desc: Refresh strategy, traffic accounting, node filtering, etc.
sync.syncTrafficHint: Pull per-user traffic from remote servers; off = accounted locally only.
sync.nodeNameFilterHint: Skip / hide upstream nodes whose name matches any of these keywords.
sync.nodeNameFilterDesc: Typically used to strip announcement nodes like 'Remaining X GB' / 'site example.com' / 'Expires yyyy-mm-dd'.
sync.appendSubInfo: Append traffic & expiry info to subscription
sync.appendSubInfoHint: Add used/remaining quota + expiry to the HTTP response header when serving subscriptions, so clients can display them.
sync.forceSyncExternalHint: Pull external subs even if the cache is still warm. Mostly for troubleshooting.
sync.matchRuleHint: Which fields define «the same node» during sync. Default is protocol+server+port (most reliable).
sync.keepNodeNameHint: Even if upstream renames a node, names you edited locally are preserved.
sync.cacheExpireHint: How long the master caches external subscriptions. Shorter = more real-time, longer = lighter load.

system.title: System options
system.desc: Short links, override scripts, mmw compat, node multiplier prefix, etc.
system.shortLinkHint: Distribute subscription URLs as /x/{code} so the user's token isn't exposed.
system.overrideScriptsHint: Run user-provided JS/Lua scripts as the last stage of subscription rendering.
system.mmwFeaturesHint: Also support the older mmw API shape and fields — helps migrating users.
system.mmwShortLinkCompatHint: Make legacy mmw two-segment short links work on mmwx so clients do not have to switch.
system.nodeMultPrefix: Add multiplier prefix to node names
system.nodeMultPrefixHint: Tag nodes whose multiplier ≠ 1 in subscription output, e.g. [×2] HK Node.

security.title: Security thresholds
security.maxAttemptsHint: Consecutive failures past this trigger a lockout. Lower = stricter.
security.lockMinutesHint: How long the account is locked once it trips the limit.
security.tokenTtlHint: How long a session credential lasts; expires → re-login. Default 168 (7 days).
security.require2faHint: When on, admins must configure TOTP before they can finish login.

turnstile.title: Cloudflare Turnstile
turnstile.desc: Add CF Turnstile (frictionless CAPTCHA) to the login page.
turnstile.enableHint: When on, the login page mounts the Turnstile widget; off = no CAPTCHA.
turnstile.siteKeyHint: Get from Cloudflare Dashboard → Turnstile. Public key, safe to expose.
turnstile.secretHint: Secret key — the master uses it to verify tokens against CF. Keep private.

schedule.title: Scheduled jobs
schedule.desc: Cron for traffic reset / cache cleanup / node sync.
schedule.trafficResetHint: When to zero users whose reset_day matures. Default: 00:00 on the 1st of each month.
schedule.cleanupHint: Clear expired sessions, logs, subscription cache. Default: 03:00 daily.
schedule.nodeSyncHint: Actively poll agents for node status. Default: every 30 minutes.

masterUrl.title: Master public URL
masterUrl.desc: URL used by subscription links, notifications, Mini App, etc.
masterUrl.label: Master URL
masterUrl.hint: Prefix for subscription links and entry point for the Telegram Mini App. Use HTTPS and make sure every user can reach it.

apiToken.title: API Token (admin)
apiToken.desc: Long-lived token for /api/admin/* calls — used by MCP / TG Bot etc.
apiToken.hint: Treat as a password — anyone with this token is admin. 👁 to reveal, 📋 to copy.

license.title: PRO license
license.desc: Activate to unlock node speed-test / rate-limit / server sharing / embedded Xray and more.
license.licenseKeyHint: Apply at mmwxlicense.com with your machine ID, paste the key here, then click Activate.
license.feat1: Node speed test — mihomo real latency + throughput
license.feat2: Node rate limit — per-node Mbps caps for upload/download
license.feat3: Server sharing — expose nodes to other mmwx instances
license.feat4: Embedded Xray — Agent embeds xray-core, no standalone binary

comparison.title: MMW vs MMW-X
comparison.description: Detailed comparison of similarities and differences between MiaoMiaoWu and MiaoMiaoWu X

overview.text1: MiaoMiaoWu X is an enhanced version of MiaoMiaoWu, inheriting all core features (subscription generation, template system, custom rules, etc.) while adding enterprise-grade capabilities like remote server management, full Xray management, certificate management, and package management.
overview.text2: In short: MiaoMiaoWu is a subscription management platform, while MiaoMiaoWu X is a complete proxy service management platform. Below is a detailed feature comparison.

nodeManagement.mmwx: UI creation + steal-self / routed outbound / tunnel

userManagement.mmw: Basic, local config still works after expiry

mmw.item1: Reads node traffic data through the probe system
mmw.item2: Can only track node-level traffic, not per-user
mmw.item3: After user expiry, local subscription configs still work since node info hasn't changed

mmwx.item1: Install MMW-Agent on servers to directly manage Xray service users and traffic
mmwx.item2: Precise traffic statistics per user and per node
mmwx.item3: Auto-revokes node access on user expiry, subscription credentials become invalid immediately
mmwx.item4: Supports traffic quotas, rate limiting, device limits, and auto-ban on expiry

mmw.item1: Manual node addition (fill in node info)
mmw.item2: Sync nodes via external subscription links
mmw.item3: Chain proxy, node grouping, sorting and basic management
mmw.item4: To add nodes on a server, you must manually log in to configure, then import to MiaoMiaoWu

mmwx.item1: Create nodes for all protocols (VLESS/VMess/Trojan/SS/Hysteria2) via UI, 19 protocol combos with one click
mmwx.item2: Steal-self: reuse existing inbound configs to quickly create new nodes
mmwx.item3: Routed Outbound: transit node reuse for traffic splitting and multi-layer forwarding
mmwx.item4: Port Forwarding Tunnel (dokodemo-door): create outbounds on existing servers, designate nodes as outbound targets
mmwx.item5: Most Xray configuration needs can be done through the UI without logging into the server

mmw.item1: Create users, assign subscription files, manage user status
mmw.item2: Supports setting user expiry, but requires manual handling after expiry
mmw.item3: Local configs still work after user expiry (node info unchanged)

mmwx.item1: Users bound to packages with traffic quota, validity period, and accessible nodes
mmwx.item2: Auto-revokes node access on expiry, credentials invalidated instantly
mmwx.item3: Rate limiting and device count limits, auto-throttle on excess
mmwx.item4: Precise traffic statistics and online user tracking

mmw.text: MiaoMiaoWu does not provide server management. All node configuration must be done manually on the server, then imported into the system.

mmwx.item1: Master-Agent architecture: control panel manages remote servers via WebSocket/HTTP/Pull
mmwx.item2: Remote install/uninstall Xray and Nginx with SSE streaming progress
mmwx.item3: Visual management of Xray inbounds, outbounds, and routing configuration
mmwx.item4: Remote online Agent upgrade and uninstall without server login
mmwx.item5: Share Server: encrypted communication (HTTPS + ECDH) for multi-user collaboration

embeddedXray.title: Embedded Xray (PRO)
embeddedXray.desc: Built-in Xray core with rate limiting, device limits, auto-throttle, online tracking, and more. No separate Xray installation needed, with hot-update capability.

routedOutbound.title: Routed Outbound
routedOutbound.desc: Node-level and per-user outbound routing. Landing node and transit node concepts for flexible traffic forwarding strategies.

shareServer.title: Share Server
shareServer.desc: Owner-Consumer model with encrypted communication (HTTPS + ECDH). Consumers have limited permissions (add inbounds, manage nodes, but cannot start/stop services or edit server settings).

certManagement.title: Certificate Management
certManagement.desc: ACME auto-apply and renew TLS certificates. Supports Cloudflare/Alibaba Cloud/Tencent Cloud/Namesilo DNS providers with auto-deployment to remote servers.

packageManagement.title: Package Management
packageManagement.desc: Create traffic packages with quota, validity, accessible nodes, and rate limit policies. Auto-managed once assigned to users.

nginxManagement.title: Nginx Management
nginxManagement.desc: Remote install/uninstall Nginx, manage config files, deploy SSL certificates, and manage Stream ports.

shared.heading: Shared Features
shared.intro: MiaoMiaoWu X inherits all core features from MiaoMiaoWu. The following features are identical in both versions:
shared.item1: Subscription generation for 12+ client formats (Clash/Surge/Shadowrocket/Loon/QX, etc.)
shared.item2: V3 template engine with flexible subscription config templates
shared.item3: Custom DNS, routing rules, and rule set management
shared.item6: Silent mode and TOTP two-factor authentication
shared.item7: Telegram push notifications and daily reports

summary.heading: Summary
summary.text1: If you only need subscription management and node import, MiaoMiaoWu is sufficient. It's simple, lightweight, and great for personal use.
summary.text2: If you need to manage multiple remote servers, precisely control user traffic and permissions, automate certificate management, or configure Xray through a UI without logging into servers, MiaoMiaoWu X is the better choice. Most of MMW-X's features are inherited from MMW, making the upgrade migration very straightforward.

toolMmwxTgbot.title: Telegram Bot (mmwX-tgbot)
toolMmwxTgbot.description: Companion Telegram bot for MiaoMiaoWu X — commands, daily notifications, and a login-free Mini App in a single binary.

overview.heading: What is it
overview.text1: mmwX-tgbot is a standalone Telegram bot with no database of its own. All data (accounts, packages, traffic, subscriptions, redeem codes) lives in your MiaoMiaoWu X master. The bot talks to /api/admin/tgbot/* on the master via a Bearer admin token; it is just the Telegram front-end.
overview.text2: So you only need a server that can reach your master's HTTP API and connect out to Telegram — no extra database, no migrations.

features.heading: Features
features.adminNote: Only TG IDs in admin_tg_ids may use these. Rate-limited to 5/min.
features.notifyText: After opt-in (/notify on) the bot pushes at 20:00 daily:
features.notifyOptin: Opt-in only; off by default; toggle stored on master.

miniapp.heading: Mini App (login-free dashboard)
miniapp.text: Open via the bot's menu button '📊 My Dashboard'. No username/password — Telegram signs initData with the bot token, the bot validates it, then trusts telegram_id and resolves the account.
miniapp.page1Desc: Account · traffic · subscription · daily chart · redeem
miniapp.warn: Mini App is a local HTTP service (127.0.0.1:23088 by default) and Telegram requires it on a public HTTPS URL. Reverse-proxy via nginx — see 'Mini App reverse proxy' below.

prereq.heading: Prerequisites
prereq.s1Text: Log into your master → System Settings → API Token. Note this must be an admin-level token, not a regular user token.
prereq.s2Text: Your master's public HTTPS address, e.g. https://mmw.domain.com. The bot must be able to reach it from where it runs.
prereq.s3Text: On Telegram, message @BotFather, send /newbot, follow the prompts. You'll get a token like 12345:AAA...
prereq.s4Text: Message @userinfobot and it'll reply with your numeric user id. Put those ids into admin_tg_ids — only listed ids can use /admin_* commands and the Mini App redeem page.

oneClick.heading: One-click install (recommended)
oneClick.text: On the target server, run the command below. The script interactively collects config (mmwx_url, mmwx_api_token, tg_bot_token, admin_tg_ids; webapp_url can be left blank for now), downloads the matching binary, installs a systemd unit, and starts the service:
oneClick.note: Config goes to /etc/mmwx-tgbot/config.yaml; service name is mmwx-tgbot. Use systemctl status mmwx-tgbot to confirm it's running, then send /start to your bot on Telegram to verify.

configFile.heading: Configuration reference
configFile.text: If you'd rather maintain config by hand, here are all the fields. Copy config.example.yaml → config.yaml and edit:
configFile.descMmwxUrl: Master URL (no trailing slash; subscription base is fixed at mmwx_url + /x)
configFile.descMmwxToken: Master admin token (System Settings → API Token)
configFile.descAdminIds: Who can use /admin_* and the Mini App redeem page (array of TG user ids)
configFile.descTimeout: HTTP timeout when calling master, in seconds
configFile.descWebappListen: Mini App local listen address (default loopback only — sit behind nginx, don't expose directly)
configFile.descWebappUrl: Mini App public HTTPS URL. If set, used as the TG menu button; leave blank to hide the entry
configFile.descWebappDev: Debug only. Keep false in production (disables ?initData= URL preview to prevent token replay via URL leaks)

envVars.heading: Environment variable overrides
envVars.text: Every yaml field can be overridden by an environment variable using the rule MMWX_TGBOT_<UPPER_SNAKE>:

systemd.heading: systemd unit
systemd.text: The one-click installer writes this unit for you. If you're deploying manually, here's a reference:

docker.heading: Docker deployment (optional)
docker.text: Prefer Docker over systemd? Bind the container port to 127.0.0.1 to match the loopback-only convention:

nginx.heading: Mini App reverse proxy (required to use the Mini App)
nginx.text1: The Mini App listens only on 127.0.0.1:23088 by default, and Telegram requires Mini Apps to be served over public HTTPS. Add these two location blocks to your master domain's nginx server, before location /:
nginx.webappUrlNote: After that, set webapp_url to https://your-domain/app, restart the bot, and the TG menu button will appear. If the bot runs on a different host, do the same reverse-proxy on that host's domain.

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.q1: The bot doesn't reply — what now?
faq.a1: First check journalctl -u mmwx-tgbot -f. Common causes: wrong tg_bot_token; the host can't reach Telegram (you may need a route out); wrong mmwx_url or invalid admin token.
faq.a2: Your TG user id isn't in admin_tg_ids. Grab your id from @userinfobot, add it to the config, then systemctl restart mmwx-tgbot.
faq.q3: Mini App opens to a blank page?
faq.a3: Make sure webapp_url is HTTPS (Telegram requires it); verify nginx reverse-proxies both /app and /api/tg-webapp/ to 127.0.0.1:23088; open DevTools in a browser to see if initData is accepted (failure returns 401).
faq.q4: How do I upgrade the bot?
faq.a4: The one-click script is idempotent — re-run it to swap the binary; config.yaml is preserved. Or stop the service, download the new binary from GitHub Releases, overwrite /usr/local/bin/mmwx-tgbot, then start it again.

toolCloudflareTurnstile.title: Cloudflare Turnstile
toolCloudflareTurnstile.description: Hook the MiaomiaowuX login page into Cloudflare Turnstile — a free, frictionless captcha that blocks brute-force attempts. Unconfigured installs gracefully skip verification — upgrades are not forced.

overview.heading: What is it
overview.text1: Cloudflare Turnstile is CF's free captcha service — no images to click, invisible to most users. It guards the login form: only after the token validates does the password check happen, dramatically reducing brute-force success rate.
overview.text2: MiaomiaowuX supports Turnstile but doesn't require it. Both keys must be filled in System Settings to enable; either empty → auto-skip. Existing installs upgrade safely.

prereq.heading: Prerequisites
prereq.text: A Cloudflare account (free tier is enough — you do NOT need to move your domain DNS to CF; Turnstile works standalone).

step1.heading: Step 1 — Create a Site in CF Dashboard
step1.intro: Log into Cloudflare Dashboard, click 'Turnstile' in the sidebar, then 'Add Site'. The table below covers the recommended fields:
step1.s1Text: Sign in with your CF account, or register a new one (free).
step1.s2Text: Find 'Turnstile' in the left sidebar (or search 'Turnstile' from the top bar). Click 'Add Site'.
step1.s3Text: Use the table below; defaults are fine for most fields:
step1.fSiteName: Anything memorable, e.g. mmwx — just for your own reference
step1.fDomain: Your MiaomiaowuX public domain (e.g. mmwx.example.com); add multiple rows if you have several
step1.fWidgetMode: Choose 'Managed' (recommended — invisible for normal users, only shows a challenge if something looks off)
step1.s4Text: After creation, the page shows 'Site Key' (public, used by the frontend widget) and 'Secret Key' (private, used by the backend siteverify). Copy both. Secret Key is shown only once — save it now.

step2.heading: Step 2 — Paste into MiaomiaowuX System Settings
step2.intro: With both keys in hand, paste them back into the MiaomiaowuX admin console.
step2.s1Text: Sign in to MiaomiaowuX as admin and open 'System Settings' from the sidebar.
step2.s2Text: Scroll to the 'Cloudflare Turnstile' card (below the 'Security thresholds' card).
step2.s3Text: Paste Site Key into the Site Key field and Secret Key into the Secret Key field. Blur the input to auto-save — no button needed, takes effect immediately without restart.

step3.heading: Step 3 — Verify it's working
step3.bullet1: Open the login page in an incognito window (to fetch the latest captcha config) — you should see the Turnstile widget at the bottom of the form (auto-pass usually within 1–2 seconds)
step3.bullet2: Sign in with correct credentials — should land on the home page, confirming the front-back wiring is good
step3.bullet3: Intentionally type a wrong password — the backend returns 401 invalid credentials; the Turnstile widget auto-resets for the next attempt

faq.heading: FAQ
faq.q1: Widget doesn't show up on the login page?
faq.a1: 1) Check Site Key is actually saved in System Settings; 2) Open DevTools → Network and verify /api/captcha/config responds with enabled:true and a non-empty site_key; 3) Domain mismatch with CF registration will block widget loading — check the browser console for errors.
faq.q2: Login is blocked by 'captcha verification failed'?
faq.a2: Secret Key is wrong, or Site Key and Secret Key don't pair. Go back to CF Dashboard, open the same Site, and re-copy both keys at once.
faq.q3: How do I replace Secret Key?
faq.a3: The Secret Key input shows a 'Configured' placeholder with an empty value for security. Just type the new Secret Key on top — blur to auto-save.
faq.a4: Empty either (or both) of the keys and save. The backend Turnstile.Enabled() returns false immediately, login no longer verifies, and the frontend stops rendering the widget.

faqNodeMgmt.title: Node creation & usage (inbound + outbound)
faqNodeMgmt.description: Five key buttons on the Node Management page, with an interactive mock that reveals each sub-UI on hover.

overview.heading: Quick orientation
overview.text: Node Management groups five capabilities on one page: «add inbound», «chained / routed outbound», «tunnel forwarding» and the global «routed outbound manager». The mock below recreates the top button strip — hover any color-ringed button to preview the sub-UI that opens.

faqNodeMgmt.intro: Each button's color ring matches the legend below; hover to see the sub-UI bubble.

mock.listSyncWarn: Heads up: edits & deletions on nodes propagate to all subscriptions
mock.speedtestComing: Speed-test dialog (not shown here; PRO feature, see Node Speed Test doc)

legend.routedRow: Blue = Add routed child on a row
legend.viewRow: Green = View routing of a node
legend.hint: Hover a ringed button to preview the sub-UI it opens

addInbound.title: Add node — Inbound wizard
addInbound.desc: Configure inbound: protocol / transport / security. Simple mode walks you through the key fields; expert mode shows the live Xray JSON.
addInbound.hint: The master auto-syncs this inbound to every subscription.

routed.title: Add routed node
routed.desc: Pick where traffic egresses; ingress is the source inbound, egress is the chosen node.
routed.scopeAllDesc: All users of the source inbound share this egress (current behaviour)
routed.scopeUserDesc: Creates a routed child node; once added to a package, subscribed users get a sub-account + rule.user automatically
routed.hint: Three tabs: pick another node, pick a server's default outbound, or pick a load balancer to fan out across egresses.

routing.title: Node routing
routing.desc: View the inbound's exclusive rules + global rules. Global rules can't be edited here — head to Custom Rules.
routing.noExclusive: No exclusive rules — traffic follows the global ruleset

tunnel.title: Tunnel manager
tunnel.desc: Manage every tunnel inbound (port-forward) across all servers. Tunnels are not listed in Nodes; delete them here.
tunnel.hint: Use case: transparently forward a port on one box to a port on another — bypassing in-between network restrictions.

routedMgr.title: Routed outbound manager
routedMgr.desc: Lists every routed child node for unified view / delete.
routedMgr.descHint: A routed child shares its parent inbound but its traffic egresses through a separate outbound. Adding it to a package auto-provisions sub-accounts + rule.user when a user binds; unsubscribing offlines but keeps credentials for renewal.

add.heading: Red — Add node (inbound)
add.text: Opens the inbound wizard. Pick a protocol (VLESS / Trojan / SS / VMess / Hysteria2 / AnyTLS / Tunnel / Socks), then transport + security. Simple mode jumps straight to the must-fill fields (REALITY domain / cert path…); expert mode shows the generated Xray JSON live.

routedRow.heading: Blue — Add routed child (inline)
routedRow.text: Inline button on a node row. Opens «Add routed node» with three add modes: pick another node as the egress / pick a server (its default outbound) / pick a load balancer (bundles multiple egresses). Scope can be «whole-node share» or «per-user isolation».

viewRow.heading: Green — View node routing (inline)
viewRow.text: Shows the effective routing for this inbound: «exclusive rules» plus the global rules (read-only here; edit them in Custom Rules).

tunnel.heading: Black — Tunnel manager
tunnel.text: Central manager for every tunnel inbound (xray «dokodemo-door»). Tunnel-type inbounds never appear in the Node list — this is the only place to delete them. Common use: transparently forward a port on box A to a port on box B.

routedAll.heading: Yellow — Routed outbound manager (global)
routedAll.text: Global view of every routed child node — review / delete / add. Each routed child shares its parent inbound credential but routes traffic through its own outbound. Add it to a package → bound users auto-provision a sub-account + rule.user, achieving «per-user routed egress» at scale.

addInbound.title: Add node
addInbound.nodeNameHint: Display name; the flag emoji is picked automatically from the server IP
addInbound.emailHint: User email identifier; empty falls back to UUID prefix

routed.title: Add routed node
routed.desc: Pick an egress node or server for "{{name}}"
routed.scopeAllDesc: All users of the source inbound share this egress (current behaviour)
routed.scopeUserDesc: Creates a routed child; once added to a package, bound users get a sub-account + rule.user automatically
routed.tabServerHint: Pick another server and use its default outbound (mock demo)
routed.tabLBHint: Bundle multiple egresses into a load balancer; traffic is split by weight / random (mock demo)
routed.searchPlaceholder: Search node name, protocol, or tag…
routed.autoExclude: Chained-proxy nodes and the source node itself are auto-excluded

routing.title: Node routing
routing.noExclusive: No exclusive rules — global rules apply

tunnel.title: Tunnel manager
tunnel.desc: Manage every tunnel (dokodemo-door) inbound across all servers. Tunnels do not appear in the node list — delete them here.

routedMgr.title: Routed outbound manager
routedMgr.explainP1: A routed outbound is a virtual node attached under a physical node — it shares the parent inbound but its traffic egresses through a separate outbound.
routedMgr.explainP2: Add this routed node to a package → users binding the package get a sub-account + rule.user automatically; unsubscribing offlines but keeps credentials for renewal.
